__inputs.name: DS_SQLSERVER
__inputs.label: SQL Server
__inputs.description: Data source Microsoft SQL Server (mssql) - configure o datasource conforme seu ambiente

description: Dashboard completo de monitoramento de SQL Server - Queries lentas, CPU, Memoria, Wait Stats, I/O, Conexoes, Database Space e Index Health

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATEDIFF(HOUR, sqlserver_start_time, GETDATE()) AS uptime_hours FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info
SELECT SERVERPROPERTY('ProductVersion') AS version
SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_connections FROM sys.dm_exec_connections
SELECT COUNT(*) AS running_requests FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE status = 'running'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS blocked_sessions FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE blocking_session_id <> 0

panels.title: Top 10 Queries Mais Lentas (por tempo medio de execucao)
panels.description: Queries ordenadas por tempo medio de execucao (total_elapsed_time / execution_count). total_elapsed_time e em microsegundos, dividido por 1000 = milissegundos. Threshold: verde < 10s, amarelo 10-35s, vermelho > 35s.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT TOP 10
    qs.execution_count,
    (qs.total_elapsed_time / qs.execution_count) / 1000 AS avg_elapsed_time_ms,
    qs.total_elapsed_time / 1000 AS total_elapsed_time_ms,
    (qs.total_worker_time / qs.execution_count) / 1000 AS avg_cpu_time_ms,
    qs.total_logical_reads / qs.execution_count AS avg_logical_reads,
    qs.total_logical_writes / qs.execution_count AS avg_logical_writes,
    qs.last_execution_time,
    SUBSTRING(st.text, (qs.statement_start_offset/2)+1,
        ((CASE qs.statement_end_offset WHEN -1 THEN DATALENGTH(st.text) ELSE qs.statement_end_offset END - qs.statement_start_offset)/2) + 1) AS query_text
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs
CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st
WHERE qs.execution_count > 0
ORDER BY (qs.total_elapsed_time / qs.execution_count) DESC

panels.title: Top 10 Queries - Tempo Medio de Execucao (ms)
panels.description: Grafico de barras horizontal mostrando o tempo medio de execucao em milissegundos das 10 queries mais lentas.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT TOP 10
    LEFT(REPLACE(REPLACE(SUBSTRING(st.text, (qs.statement_start_offset/2)+1,
        ((CASE qs.statement_end_offset WHEN -1 THEN DATALENGTH(st.text) ELSE qs.statement_end_offset END - qs.statement_start_offset)/2) + 1), CHAR(13), ' '), CHAR(10), ' '), 60) AS query_short,
    (qs.total_elapsed_time / qs.execution_count) / 1000 AS avg_elapsed_time_ms
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs
CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st
WHERE qs.execution_count > 0
ORDER BY (qs.total_elapsed_time / qs.execution_count) DESC

panels.title: Query Mais Lenta (avg ms)
panels.description: Gauge da query com maior tempo medio de execucao. Verde < 10s, Amarelo 10-35s, Vermelho > 35s.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT TOP 1
    (qs.total_elapsed_time / qs.execution_count) / 1000 AS max_avg_elapsed_time_ms
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs
WHERE qs.execution_count > 0
ORDER BY (qs.total_elapsed_time / qs.execution_count) DESC

panels.title: CPU Usage ao Longo do Tempo
panels.description: Historico de CPU do SQL Server vs outros processos (ultimos ~60 registros do ring buffer).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT TOP 60
    DATEADD(ms, -1 * ((SELECT cpu_ticks / (cpu_ticks / ms_ticks) FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) - [timestamp]), GETDATE()) AS time,
    SQLProcessUtilization AS sql_cpu_percent,
    SystemIdle AS system_idle_percent,
    100 - SystemIdle - SQLProcessUtilization AS other_cpu_percent
FROM (
    SELECT
        record.value('(./Record/@id)[1]', 'int') AS record_id,
        record.value('(./Record/SchedulerMonitorEvent/SystemHealth/SystemIdle)[1]', 'int') AS SystemIdle,
        record.value('(./Record/SchedulerMonitorEvent/SystemHealth/ProcessUtilization)[1]', 'int') AS SQLProcessUtilization,
        [timestamp]
    FROM (
        SELECT [timestamp], CONVERT(xml, record) AS record
        FROM sys.dm_os_ring_buffers
        WHERE ring_buffer_type = N'RING_BUFFER_SCHEDULER_MONITOR'
        AND record LIKE N'%<SystemHealth>%'
    ) AS x
) AS y
ORDER BY record_id DESC
SELECT total_physical_memory_kb / 1024 AS total_memory_mb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_memory
SELECT physical_memory_in_use_kb / 1024 AS sql_memory_mb FROM sys.dm_os_process_memory

panels.title: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio
panels.description: Percentual de paginas encontradas no buffer cache sem necessidade de leitura em disco. Deve ser > 95% em ambientes OLTP.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    CAST((a.cntr_value * 1.0 / NULLIF(b.cntr_value, 0)) * 100 AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS buffer_cache_hit_ratio
FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters a
JOIN sys.dm_os_performance_counters b ON a.object_name = b.object_name
WHERE a.counter_name = 'Buffer cache hit ratio'
AND b.counter_name = 'Buffer cache hit ratio base'
AND a.object_name LIKE '%Buffer Manager%'

panels.title: Page Life Expectancy
panels.description: Tempo em segundos que uma pagina permanece no buffer pool sem ser referenciada. Bom: > 300s. Valores baixos indicam pressao de memoria.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT cntr_value AS page_life_expectancy_sec
FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters
WHERE counter_name = 'Page life expectancy'
AND object_name LIKE '%Buffer Manager%'

panels.title: ROW 4: Wait Stats (Onde o SQL Perde Tempo)
Top 10 Wait Types (Pie Chart)
panels.description: Distribuicao dos wait types mais significativos. Exclui waits de sistema (idle, sleep, etc.).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT TOP 10
    wait_type,
    wait_time_ms / 1000 AS wait_time_sec
FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats
WHERE wait_type NOT IN (
    'SLEEP_TASK', 'BROKER_TO_FLUSH', 'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',
    'CLR_AUTO_EVENT', 'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT', 'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',
    'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE', 'WAITFOR', 'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',
    'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT', 'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',
    'XE_TIMER_EVENT', 'HADR_FILESTREAM_IOMGR_IOCOMPLETION',
    'DIRTY_PAGE_POLL', 'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',
    'LOGMGR_QUEUE', 'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE', 'BROKER_RECEIVE_WAITFOR',
    'PREEMPTIVE_OS_GETPROCADDRESS', 'PREEMPTIVE_OS_AUTHENTICATIONOPS',
    'BROKER_TASK_STOP', 'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP'
)
AND waiting_tasks_count > 0
ORDER BY wait_time_ms DESC

panels.title: Detalhes dos Wait Stats
panels.description: Tabela detalhada com wait types, tempo total de espera, signal wait e media por task.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT TOP 15
    wait_type,
    wait_time_ms / 1000 AS wait_time_sec,
    signal_wait_time_ms / 1000 AS signal_wait_sec,
    (wait_time_ms - signal_wait_time_ms) / 1000 AS resource_wait_sec,
    waiting_tasks_count,
    CASE WHEN waiting_tasks_count > 0
        THEN CAST(wait_time_ms * 1.0 / waiting_tasks_count AS DECIMAL(12,2))
        ELSE 0
    END AS avg_wait_ms
FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats
WHERE wait_type NOT IN (
    'SLEEP_TASK', 'BROKER_TO_FLUSH', 'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',
    'CLR_AUTO_EVENT', 'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT', 'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',
    'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE', 'WAITFOR', 'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',
    'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT', 'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',
    'XE_TIMER_EVENT', 'HADR_FILESTREAM_IOMGR_IOCOMPLETION',
    'DIRTY_PAGE_POLL', 'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',
    'LOGMGR_QUEUE', 'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE', 'BROKER_RECEIVE_WAITFOR',
    'PREEMPTIVE_OS_GETPROCADDRESS', 'PREEMPTIVE_OS_AUTHENTICATIONOPS',
    'BROKER_TASK_STOP', 'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP'
)
AND waiting_tasks_count > 0
ORDER BY wait_time_ms DESC

panels.title: I/O por Database (Top Databases por Stall)
panels.description: Mostra total de leituras, escritas, volumes e latencia (stall) por database. Latencia alta indica problemas de disco.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    DB_NAME(database_id) AS database_name,
    SUM(num_of_reads) AS total_reads,
    SUM(num_of_writes) AS total_writes,
    SUM(num_of_bytes_read) / 1024 / 1024 AS total_read_mb,
    SUM(num_of_bytes_written) / 1024 / 1024 AS total_write_mb,
    SUM(io_stall_read_ms) AS read_stall_ms,
    SUM(io_stall_write_ms) AS write_stall_ms,
    CASE WHEN SUM(num_of_reads) > 0
        THEN CAST(SUM(io_stall_read_ms) * 1.0 / SUM(num_of_reads) AS DECIMAL(10,2))
        ELSE 0
    END AS avg_read_latency_ms,
    CASE WHEN SUM(num_of_writes) > 0
        THEN CAST(SUM(io_stall_write_ms) * 1.0 / SUM(num_of_writes) AS DECIMAL(10,2))
        ELSE 0
    END AS avg_write_latency_ms
FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL)
WHERE DB_NAME(database_id) IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY database_id
ORDER BY (SUM(io_stall_read_ms) + SUM(io_stall_write_ms)) DESC

panels.title: Avg Disk Read Latency (Global)
panels.description: Latencia media de leitura em disco (todas as databases). Bom < 20ms, Alerta > 50ms.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    CASE WHEN SUM(num_of_reads) > 0
        THEN CAST(SUM(io_stall_read_ms) * 1.0 / SUM(num_of_reads) AS DECIMAL(10,2))
        ELSE 0
    END AS avg_read_latency_ms
FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL)

panels.title: Avg Disk Write Latency (Global)
panels.description: Latencia media de escrita em disco (todas as databases). Bom < 20ms, Alerta > 50ms.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    CASE WHEN SUM(num_of_writes) > 0
        THEN CAST(SUM(io_stall_write_ms) * 1.0 / SUM(num_of_writes) AS DECIMAL(10,2))
        ELSE 0
    END AS avg_write_latency_ms
FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL)

panels.title: Sessoes Ativas (User Processes)
panels.description: Lista de sessoes de usuario com CPU, memoria, leituras, escritas e informacoes de bloqueio.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    s.session_id,
    s.login_name,
    s.host_name,
    DB_NAME(s.database_id) AS database_name,
    s.status,
    s.cpu_time,
    s.memory_usage * 8 AS memory_kb,
    s.reads,
    s.writes,
    s.last_request_start_time,
    r.command,
    r.wait_type,
    r.wait_time AS wait_time_ms,
    ISNULL(r.blocking_session_id, 0) AS blocking_session_id,
    CASE WHEN r.sql_handle IS NOT NULL
        THEN (SELECT TOP 1 LEFT(text, 100) FROM sys.dm_exec_sql_text(r.sql_handle))
        ELSE NULL
    END AS current_query
FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions s
LEFT JOIN sys.dm_exec_requests r ON s.session_id = r.session_id
WHERE s.is_user_process = 1
ORDER BY s.cpu_time DESC

panels.title: Sessoes Bloqueadas (AGORA)
panels.description: Numero de sessoes que estao sendo bloqueadas por outras sessoes neste momento. Qualquer valor > 0 e preocupante.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS blocked_sessions
FROM sys.dm_exec_requests
WHERE blocking_session_id <> 0

panels.title: Blocking Chains (Detalhes)
panels.description: Detalhes de sessoes bloqueadas e suas blockers. Mostra quem esta bloqueando quem, ha quanto tempo e qual query esta envolvida.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    r.session_id AS blocked_session_id,
    r.blocking_session_id AS blocker_session_id,
    DB_NAME(r.database_id) AS database_name,
    r.wait_type,
    r.wait_time / 1000.0 AS wait_time_sec,
    r.command,
    s_blocked.login_name AS blocked_login,
    s_blocked.host_name AS blocked_host,
    s_blocker.login_name AS blocker_login,
    s_blocker.host_name AS blocker_host,
    LEFT((SELECT TOP 1 text FROM sys.dm_exec_sql_text(r.sql_handle)), 120) AS blocked_query,
    LEFT(
        (SELECT TOP 1 text FROM sys.dm_exec_sql_text(
            (SELECT TOP 1 r2.sql_handle FROM sys.dm_exec_requests r2 WHERE r2.session_id = r.blocking_session_id)
        )), 120
    ) AS blocker_query
FROM sys.dm_exec_requests r
JOIN sys.dm_exec_sessions s_blocked ON r.session_id = s_blocked.session_id
LEFT JOIN sys.dm_exec_sessions s_blocker ON r.blocking_session_id = s_blocker.session_id
WHERE r.blocking_session_id <> 0
ORDER BY r.wait_time DESC

panels.title: Tamanho dos Arquivos de Database
panels.description: Tamanho atual, limite maximo e crescimento configurado de cada arquivo de cada database.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    DB_NAME(database_id) AS database_name,
    type_desc,
    name AS file_name,
    physical_name,
    size * 8 / 1024 AS size_mb,
    CASE max_size
        WHEN -1 THEN 'Unlimited'
        WHEN 0 THEN 'No Growth'
        ELSE CAST(max_size * 8 / 1024 AS VARCHAR) + ' MB'
    END AS max_size,
    CASE is_percent_growth
        WHEN 1 THEN CAST(growth AS VARCHAR) + ' %'
        ELSE CAST(growth * 8 / 1024 AS VARCHAR) + ' MB'
    END AS growth_config
FROM sys.master_files
ORDER BY size DESC

panels.title: Top Databases por Tamanho Total
panels.description: Grafico de barras horizontal mostrando o tamanho total de cada database (DATA + LOG).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    DB_NAME(database_id) AS database_name,
    SUM(CASE WHEN type_desc = 'ROWS' THEN size * 8 / 1024 ELSE 0 END) AS data_mb,
    SUM(CASE WHEN type_desc = 'LOG' THEN size * 8 / 1024 ELSE 0 END) AS log_mb
FROM sys.master_files
GROUP BY database_id
ORDER BY SUM(size) DESC

panels.title: Missing Indexes - Maior Impacto de Melhoria
panels.description: Indices que o SQL Server sugere criar. Ordenado por improvement_measure (combinacao de custo, impacto e frequencia de seeks).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT TOP 15
    DB_NAME(d.database_id) AS database_name,
    OBJECT_NAME(d.object_id, d.database_id) AS table_name,
    d.equality_columns,
    d.inequality_columns,
    d.included_columns,
    gs.user_seeks,
    gs.user_scans,
    CAST(gs.avg_total_user_cost AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS avg_total_user_cost,
    CAST(gs.avg_user_impact AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS avg_user_impact_pct,
    CAST(gs.avg_total_user_cost * gs.avg_user_impact * (gs.user_seeks + gs.user_scans) AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS improvement_measure
FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details d
JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups g ON d.index_handle = g.index_handle
JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats gs ON g.index_group_handle = gs.group_handle
WHERE DB_NAME(d.database_id) IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY gs.avg_total_user_cost * gs.avg_user_impact * (gs.user_seeks + gs.user_scans) DESC

panels.title: Indices Mais Fragmentados (> 30%)
panels.description: Indices com fragmentacao alta. Fragmentacao > 30% requer REBUILD, entre 10-30% requer REORGANIZE. Apenas indices com mais de 1000 paginas sao relevantes.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
    DB_NAME() AS database_name,
    OBJECT_NAME(ips.object_id) AS table_name,
    i.name AS index_name,
    i.type_desc AS index_type,
    CAST(ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS avg_frag_pct,
    ips.page_count,
    ips.record_count,
    CASE
        WHEN ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30 THEN 'REBUILD'
        WHEN ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 10 THEN 'REORGANIZE'
        ELSE 'OK'
    END AS recommended_action
FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, 'LIMITED') ips
JOIN sys.indexes i ON ips.object_id = i.object_id AND ips.index_id = i.index_id
WHERE ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 10
AND ips.page_count > 1000
AND i.name IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent DESC

title: SQL Server Performance Monitor - PRD
